name: status falls back to the server default approval mode
judgeRubric: A fresh session with no stored override should report the server default approval mode and list the available approval modes.

name: status clamps a stored session mode to the stricter server default
judgeRubric: When a stored approval override is less strict than the server default, the status response must report the stricter effective mode instead of the raw stored value.

name: update uses the query session id when the request body omits it
judgeRubric: A POST approval update should apply the requested mode to the query-string sessionId when the request body omits sessionId, return success, and leave the default session unchanged.

name: update clamps a looser body mode to the stricter server default
judgeRubric: If the request body asks for a less restrictive mode than the server default, the persisted session mode and response should remain at the stricter default.

name: update prefers the body session id over the query string
judgeRubric: If both body and query sessionId values are present, the body sessionId must win and only that target session should reflect the updated approval mode.

name: request resolution uses the stored session approval mode when no header is set
judgeRubric: Request-time approval resolution should use a stored session mode when no explicit header override is present.

name: request resolution lets an explicit header override the stored session mode
judgeRubric: An explicit approval header must override any stored session mode for the current request.

name: request resolution honors an auto header override
judgeRubric: The resolver must not let an explicit auto header override relax a stricter server default.

name: request resolution falls back to the server default when no session mode exists
judgeRubric: If there is no stored session override and no header override, request resolution should fall back to the server default approval mode.
